message.abstract:
<jats:p>
                  It is not currently known how long it takes a person infected by the COVID-19 virus to become infectious. Models of the spread of COVID-19 use very different lengths for this latency period, leading to very different estimates of the replication number
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  , even when models work from the same underlying data sets. In this paper we quantify how much varying the length of the latency period affects estimates of
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  , and thus the fraction of the population that is predicted to be infected in the first wave of the pandemic. This variation underscores the uncertainty in our understanding of
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  and raises the possibility that
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  may be considerably greater than has been assumed by those shaping public policy.
                </jats:p>
title: Effects of latency on estimates of the COVID-19 replication number

reference.unstructured: Zhanwei Du , Xiaoke Xu , Y. Wu 3, Lin Wang , Benjamin J. Cowling, and Lauren Ancel Meyers, The serial interval of COVID-19 from publicly reported confirmed cases, MedRxiv 2020.
“Infected but feeling fine. The unwitting coronavirus spreaders,” New York Times, March 31, 2020.

Remy Pasco , Xutong Wang , Michaela Petty , Zhanwei Du , Spencer J. Fox , Michael Pignone , Clay Johnston , Lauren Ancel Meyers , COVID-19 Health-care Demand Projections: Austin, Texas, presentation to the Austin City Council, March 24, 2020. https://sites.cns.utexas.edu/sites/default/files/cid/files/covid-19analysisforaustinmarch2020.pdf

Neil M Ferguson , Daniel Laydon , Gemma Nedjati-Gilani , Natsuko Imai , Kylie Ainslie , Marc Baguelin , Sangeeta Bhatia , Adhiratha Boonyasiri , Zulma Cucunubá , Gina Cuomo-Dannenburg , Amy Dighe , Ilaria Dorigatti , Han Fu , Katy Gaythorpe , Will Green , Arran Hamlet , Wes Hinsley , Lucy C Okell , Sabine van Elsland , Hayley Thompson , Robert Verity , Erik Volz , Haowei Wang , Yuanrong Wang , Patrick GT Walker , Caroline Walters , Peter Winskill , Charles Whittaker , Christl A Donnelly , Steven Riley , Azra C Ghani , Impact of non-pharmaceutical interventions (NPIs) to reduce COVID19 mortality and healthcare demand, DOI: https://doi.org/10.25561/77482

Finer subdivisions, such as regions of China, may be found at https://interaktiv.morgenpost.de/corona-virus-karte-infektionen-deutschland-weltweit/
For a very clear introduction to the SIR model and its variants, including SEIR, see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compartmentalmodelsinepidemiology
